params.name: Erasmo of Narni
params.description:
On May 16, 1450 AD, the Erasmo of Narni was placed in front of a church in Padua.
This sculpture is the work of Donatello, the first master sculptor of the Renaissance. At that time, the Venetian Republic commissioned this master to create a sculpture to commemorate the achievements of Gatamelata. The sculptor created a life-size figure that is in complete harmony with the large and heavy horse beneath, conveying a sense of full power that not only impressed people but also became the object …
